answer:
We need to evaluate \(\lim_{x \to 0} \left( x \left\lfloor \frac{1}{x} \right\rfloor \right)\).  
For \(x > 0\), we have \(\frac{1}{x} - 1 < \left\lfloor \frac{1}{x} \right\rfloor \le \frac{1}{x}\), so multiplying by \(x\) gives \(1 - x < x \left\lfloor \frac{1}{x} \right\rfloor \le 1\).  
By the squeeze theorem, as \(x \to 0^+\), the expression approaches 1; the same limit holds from the left, so the limit is 1.

\boxed{B}
